style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Global Context" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="-560" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-7" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;align=left;" value="<p>When <code>this</code> is used outside of any function or object, it refers to the global object.</p><ul><li>In a <strong>browser</strong> environment, the global object is <code>window</code>.</li><li>In <strong>Node.js</strong>, the global object is <code>global</code>.</li></ul>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="120" width="280" x="-560" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-11" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Function Context (Non-strict mode)" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="-240" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-12" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<br><div style="text-align: left;"><span style="background-color: initial;">When </span><code style="background-color: initial;">this</code><span style="background-color: initial;"> is used inside a </span><strong style="background-color: initial;">regular function</strong><span style="background-color: initial;">, it refers to the global object (in non-strict mode) unless otherwise specified.</span></div><div style="text-align: left;"><br></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="240" x="-240" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-14" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;align=left;" value="In <strong>strict mode</strong>, however, <code>this</code> is <code>undefined</code> inside a function, unless set explicitly." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="280" x="-240" y="480" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-15" parent="1" style="text;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="
<div style="color: #cccccc;background-color: #1f1f1f;font-family: Consolas, 'Courier New', monospace;font-weight: normal;font-size: 14px;line-height: 19px;white-space: pre;"><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #9cdcfe;">console</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">.</span><span style="color: #dcdcaa;">log</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">(</span><span style="color: #569cd6;">this</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">); </span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// In a browser,</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp;</span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// &nbsp;this will log the `window` object</span></div></div>

" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="430" x="-560" y="365" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-17" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Method Context (Inside an Object)" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="70" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-18" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;align=left;" value="When <code>this</code> is used inside a <strong>method</strong> of an object, it refers to the object itself." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="270" x="60" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-23" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Arrow Functions" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="360" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-24" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;align=left;" value="Arrow functions behave differently. They <strong>do not have their own <code>this</code></strong>. Instead, they inherit <code>this</code> from the surrounding lexical scope." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="290" x="360" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-26" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Event Handlers" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="710" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-27" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;align=left;" value="In event handlers, <code>this</code> refers to the element that fired the event.<div><p>In the below example, <code>this</code> will refer to the <code>&lt;button&gt;</code> element.</p><p>Alternatively, you can set up an event listener in JavaScript:</p><div><br></div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="140" width="250" x="710" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <UserObject label="
<div style="color: #cccccc;background-color: #1f1f1f;font-family: Consolas, 'Courier New', monospace;font-weight: normal;font-size: 14px;line-height: 19px;white-space: pre;"><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #808080;">&lt;</span><span style="color: #569cd6;">button</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> </span><span style="color: #9cdcfe;">id</span><span style="color: #d4d4d4;">=</span><span style="color: #ce9178;">"myButton"</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp;</span><span style="color: #9cdcfe;">onclick</span><span style="color: #d4d4d4;">=</span><span style="color: #ce9178;">"console.log(this)"</span><span style="color: #808080;">&gt;</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; Click me</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp;</span><span style="color: #808080;">&lt;/</span><span style="color: #569cd6;">button</span><span style="color: #808080;">&gt;</span></div></div>

" link="
<div style="color: #cccccc;background-color: #1f1f1f;font-family: Consolas, 'Courier New', monospace;font-weight: normal;font-size: 14px;line-height: 19px;white-space: pre;"><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #808080;">&lt;</span><span style="color: #569cd6;">button</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> </span><span style="color: #9cdcfe;">id</span><span style="color: #d4d4d4;">=</span><span style="color: #ce9178;">"myButton"</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp;</span><span style="color: #9cdcfe;">onclick</span><span style="color: #d4d4d4;">=</span><span style="color: #ce9178;">"console.log(this)"</span><span style="color: #808080;">&gt;</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; Click me</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp;</span><span style="color: #808080;">&lt;/</span><span style="color: #569cd6;">button</span><span style="color: #808080;">&gt;</span></div></div>

}</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #569cd6;">const</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> </span><span style="color: #4fc1ff;">alice</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> </span><span style="color: #d4d4d4;">=</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> </span><span style="color: #569cd6;">new</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> </span><span style="color: #4ec9b0;">Person</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">(</span><span style="color: #ce9178;">"Alice"</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">);</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// Logs "Alice"</span></div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="140" width="360" x="1360" y="340" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-33" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="Class Context" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="1720" y="160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-34" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;align=left;" value="In <strong>JavaScript classes</strong>, <code>this</code> refers to the instance of the class." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="65" width="320" x="1720" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="d6LM5-7s0fH1nzZqCMt4-35" parent="1" style="text;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div style="color: #cccccc;background-color: #1f1f1f;font-family: Consolas, 'Courier New', monospace;font-weight: normal;font-size: 14px;line-height: 19px;white-space: pre;"><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp;</span><span style="color: #569cd6;">class</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> </span><span style="color: #4ec9b0;">Person</span><span style="color: #cccccc;"> {</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; }</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; </span><span style="color: #dcdcaa;">greet</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">() {</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// Refers to `window` in a browser</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; }</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; </span><span style="color: #dcdcaa;">greet</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">() {</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; </span><span style="color: #9cdcfe;">console</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">.</span><span style="color: #dcdcaa;">log</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">(</span><span style="color: #569cd6;">this</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">.</span><span style="color: #9cdcfe;">name</span><span style="color: #cccccc;">);</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// Refers to `person` object,</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// logs "Alice"</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// `this` is undefined</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">}</span></div><br></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="160" width="220" x="-240" y="540" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <UserObject label="<div style="color: #cccccc;background-color: #1f1f1f;font-family: Consolas, 'Courier New', monospace;font-weight: normal;font-size: 14px;line-height: 19px;white-space: pre;"><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// Inherits `this` from</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; </span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// the outer scope (e.g., global),</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;</span><span style="color: #6a9955;">// &nbsp;undefined in strict mode</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; &nbsp; }</span></div><div><span style="color: #cccccc;">&nbsp; div.div: Global Context

div.p: When this is used outside of any function or object, it refers to the global object.

ul.li: In a browser environment, the global object is window.
In Node.js, the global object is global.

div.div: Arrow functions behave differently. They do not have their own this. Instead, they inherit this from the surrounding lexical scope.

div.div:
In event handlers, this refers to the element that fired the event.
In the below example, this will refer to the <button> element.
Alternatively, you can set up an event listener in JavaScript:

https://app.diagrams.net/
  <button id="myButton"
   onclick="console.log(this)">
    Click me
 </button>

https://app.diagrams.net/
  const person = {
    name: "Alice",
    greet: () => {
      console.log(this.name);
      // Inherits `this` from
      // the outer scope (e.g., global),
     //  undefined in strict mode
    }
  };
 
  person.greet();
  // `this.name` is undefined

div.div: In JavaScript, this is a special keyword that often refers to the context in which a function is executed. The value of this depends on how and where a function is called, which can make it tricky but also quite powerful

rect.style: fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.div: Common Pitfalls

div.div: 1. Losing this context in callbacks:

rect.style: fill: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 248px; height: 1px; padding-top: 335px; margin-left: 2682px;

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: If a function is passed as a callback, this may lose its intended context. This is common with asynchronous code.

div.p: You can fix this by using an arrow function, which will inherit this from greet().

ul.li: You can now use this in object methods in ES6, but if you use an arrow function in an object literal, this may not refer to the expected object.
